title: Advanced modality and stance
explanation:
Advanced modality and stance

C1 speakers express stance with nuanced modals and semi-modals: be bound to, be likely to, be supposed to, would rather, had better.

questions.text: Given the evidence, the jury [*is bound to|is never going to|is forbidden to] reach a verdict soon.
You [*would rather|would better|would sooner not] discuss this in public, wouldn't you?
We [*had better|would rather|must better] leave before the storm arrives.
The package [*is supposed to|is bound to|is likely not to] arrive today, but tracking says otherwise.
She [*would sooner resign|would better resign|had rather resign] than compromise her principles.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The policy is likely ____ face legal challenges. | *to | for | that
He is ____ to know the answer; he wrote the report. | *bound | afraid | slow
You ____ not have interrupted the speaker. | *should | would | might
I ____ rather you didn't share that document. | *would | had | must
They [*are unlikely to|are likely to|are bound to] abandon the project entirely.
You [*had better not|would rather not|are supposed not] forget the deadline.
He [*is meant to|is meaning to|is minded to] chair the meeting tomorrow.
The outcome [*could hardly have been|could easily have been|must always be] worse.
I [*would just as soon|would rather just|had better just] stay at home tonight.
questions.feedback: Would just as soon = would rather.
